tools.summary: Blueprint Builder sub-turn only (agent_surfaces: blueprint_builder; not on default main Agent). Compile a Blueprint and return diagnostics. Use after blueprint_graph_patch, blueprint_graph_patch, or other graph edits to validate/fix compile state; not for graph discovery (use blueprint_get_graph_summary or blueprint_graph_introspect). Use format_graphs to run in-process graph layout on script graphs before compile when graphs should be laid out.

format_graphs.description: When true, run bundled graph layout on all non-empty ubergraph, function, and macro graphs before compiling.

focused.description: After success, open and foreground this Blueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Blueprint Builder sub-turn only (agent_surfaces: blueprint_builder; not on default main Agent). Atomic apply: any op failure cancels the transaction (applied_partial always []). Returns patch_errors or patch_validate_errors with errors[], error_codes[], errors_detail[] (structured entries), failed_op_index, expected_vs_actual on blueprint_parent_class_mismatch, suggested_correct_call, optional available_pins_from/to, guid_found_in_graphs. Semantic preflight rejects create_node ops whose event_override outer or non-static call_function class is not a superclass of this Blueprint's ParentClass (e.g. Character Jump on PlayerController asset). Non-validate apply runs internal validate-only simulation first (Editor setting; skip_internal_validate_before_apply for tests). validate_only: transient create_node spawns—do not reuse those GUIDs; use patch_id in-batch or introspect after apply. Max ops per call from Editor settings (patch_batch_too_large). Node refs: patch_id, guid:…, or bare UUID (no __UAI_G_* placeholders—see prompt 07-graph-patch-canonical). Resolver preflight: k2_class aliases, gameplay UClass mistaken for k2→class_path+call_library_function, op call_function→create_node, semantic_kind call_function typo, event_override hoisting, Character Jump/Landed paths, connect/connect_exec aliases, add_variable types, layout_scope/anchor aliases. Large batches: ops_json_path under Saved/ or harness_step/. Ops: create_node, create_comment, connect, connect_exec, break_link, splice_on_link, set_pin_default, add_variable, remove_node, move_node. auto_layout + layout_scope (patched_nodes | patched_nodes_and_downstream_exec | full_graph) + layout_anchor (neighbor | below_existing). Post-patch Sugiyama-style layout; formatting prefs in Editor → Unreal AI Editor → Blueprint Formatting.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset object path under /Game only (e.g. /Game/Folder/MyBP.MyBP).

graph_name.description: Script graph to edit (default: EventGraph). Use export/summary to list function or macro graphs.

compile.description: When true (default), recompile the Blueprint after ops. For full compiler_messages[], follow with blueprint_compile.

auto_layout.description: When true (default), run bundled graph layout after ops succeed. Set false to preserve manual node positions for this patch.

layout_scope.description: patched_nodes: layout only nodes created in this patch (create_node/create_comment), then translate cluster toward wired neighbors and push overlapping non-patch script nodes right (AABB + gap). patched_nodes_and_downstream_exec: same seeds plus BFS along exec outputs within the graph (max 384 nodes total); use after splice/break/interject so downstream tails reflow; per-node x/y hints from create_node are ignored for that pass (layout_hints_ignored). full_graph: LayoutEntireGraph—explicit whole-graph reformat. Aliases repaired to patched_nodes_and_downstream_exec: patched_plus_downstream, downstream, patch_and_tail.

layout_anchor.description: neighbor (default): after local layout, translate the cluster toward exec-linked neighbors (existing behavior). below_existing: before layout, shift the laid-out node set downward so its top clears the bottom of existing script nodes plus margin (Autonomix-style free-floating subgraph placement). Ignored when layout_scope is full_graph. Alias repaired: below → below_existing.

validate_only.description: When true, validate ops[] without persisting: create_node instances are spawned on the graph then removed. connect between two batch-local nodes simulates TryCreateConnection; if either endpoint is a saved graph node, only pin resolution and output→input direction are checked (wires on saved nodes are not altered).

skip_internal_validate_before_apply.description: When true and validate_only is false, skip the automatic validate-only dry-run before opening the edit transaction (automation only; normal agent calls should omit this).

ops_json_path.description: Project-relative path to a UTF-8 JSON file whose root value is an array of patch op objects (same schema as ops[]). Must start with Saved/ or harness_step/. Mutually exclusive with non-empty inline ops[]; use for very large patches that should not be embedded in the tool-call stream.

ops.description: Ordered operations (non-empty unless ops_json_path is set). NodeRef for connect/break_link/splice: patch_id from this batch, guid:uuid, or bare node_guid from blueprint_graph_introspect. `self` is not a graph node—use blueprint_graph_list_pins for VariableSet Target pins.

patch_id.description: Local id for NodeRef in connect/set_pin_default within this ops list (e.g. n1).

k2_class.description: UK2Node class object path, e.g. /Script/BlueprintGraph.K2Node_IfThenElse. Omit when using semantic_kind instead. If both are set, k2_class wins. Use K2Node_ prefix in /Script paths (not UK2Node_). Bare K2Node_* expands to /Script/BlueprintGraph.* (K2Node_Enhanced* → /Script/InputBlueprintNodes.*). Integer math/compare: K2Node_CallFunction + /Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary + Less_IntInt / Add_IntInt / etc. Do not put node_guid on create_node. Connect refs must be real GUIDs or patch_id (invalid T3D-style tokens rejected).

semantic_kind.description: Closed set (do not invent): branch | execution_sequence | call_library_function | variable_get | variable_set | event_override (alias event) | custom_event | dynamic_cast. Not valid: literal_int / literal_float / call_function as semantic_kind — use call_library_function + /Script/Engine.KismetSystemLibrary + MakeLiteralInt (etc.) + set_pin_default on patch_id.Value; resolver may rewrite common literal_* mistakes and KismetMathLibrary+MakeLiteral* to SystemLibrary. Required when k2_class is omitted. Must appear in this object for resolver schema validation (additionalProperties: false).

event_name.description: For K2Node_CustomEvent: function name (also custom_event_name or custom_event.name or name).

name.description: Optional display/identifier; for K2Node_CustomEvent used as event name if event_name empty.

class_path.description: For K2Node_CallFunction: owning UClass of the UFunction (e.g. /Script/Engine.KismetSystemLibrary). Use with function_name at the top level, or use call_function { class_path, function_name } instead.

function_name.description: For K2Node_CallFunction: UFunction short name (e.g. PrintString). Required with class_path unless call_function object is used.
Event or function name on outer class (e.g. Landed, Jump, ReceiveBeginPlay).

outer_class_path.description: Class that declares the member (e.g. /Script/Engine.Character for Landed/Jump). Optional for several common lifecycle names — the engine defaults outer_class when omitted; still set explicitly when unsure.

variable_name.description: Blueprint member name for K2Node_VariableGet / K2Node_VariableSet.

type.description: Member pin type: string token (int, float, bool, byte, int64, name, text, vector, rotator, or /Script/... /Game/... class or struct path) or object { category or pin_category, optional subcategory, class_path or subcategory_object for object/struct }.

from.description: Output pin NodeRef.Pin (patchId.pin, guid:uuid.pin, or export node_guid.pin). Must resolve to an OUTPUT pin; `to` must be an INPUT. Reversed refs fail with an explicit error (no silent swap). Prefer pin `name` from blueprint_graph_introspect. Aliases: Execute/Exec/Execution, True→Then, False→Else, inputObject→Target, returnValue→ReturnValue.

to.description: Input pin NodeRef.Pin. Must resolve to an INPUT pin. Do not use `self` as a node id.

link_from.description: Resolver folds into `from` before validation when `from` is omitted (same NodeRef.Pin format).

link_to.description: Resolver folds into `to` before validation when `to` is omitted.

from_node.description: Optional split ref: combined with `from_pin` into `from` by resolver (then removed).

to_node.description: Optional split ref: combined with `to_pin` into `to` by resolver.

from_node.description: Node ref only (patch_id from this batch, guid:uuid, or bare UUID). Must not contain '.'; use op connect for explicit pins.

to_node.description: Node ref only for the exec input target.

from.description: Alias of from_node when to is set (both must be node-only refs).

to.description: Alias of to_node when from is set.

node_ref.description: Alias of ref: same NodeRef.Pin string (guid.pin or patchId.pin).

node_guid.description: Graph node id; plain UUID or guid: prefix accepted.

value.description: Default string as editor expects for this pin category.

default_value.description: Alias of value (models often use this name); resolver copies to value before apply.

from.description: NodeRef.Pin of one end of an existing link.

link_from.description: Resolver folds into `from` when `from` is omitted.

link_to.description: Resolver folds into `to` when `to` is omitted.

from.description: Upstream pin (typically exec out e.g. .Then). Alias: link_from if from is omitted.

to.description: Downstream pin (typically exec in e.g. .Execute). Alias: link_to if to is omitted.

link_from.description: Alias of from for splice_on_link / break_link / connect.

link_to.description: Alias of to for splice_on_link / break_link / connect.

insert_patch_id.description: patch_id of a node created earlier in this ops list.

member_node_refs.description: Node ids (patch_id or guid:uuid) to bound after layout—no .Pin suffix.

tools.ue_entry_points: UBlueprint; UEdGraph; UEdGraphSchema_K2; FUnrealBlueprintGraphFormatService; UnrealBlueprintCommentReflow; FKismetEditorUtilities::CompileBlueprint
failure_modes: ops_json_path must be project-relative under Saved/ or harness_step/; file root must be a non-empty JSON array of op objects; mutually exclusive with non-empty inline ops[].
Unknown op or invalid fields per op (see parameters schema).
k2_class missing or not a UK2Node; symbol not found for call_function.
connect: `from` must be an output pin and `to` an input pin (swap if the error says dir=in/out); ambiguous duplicate pin display names fail deterministically.
Connection rejected (pin types); inspect pins with blueprint_graph_list_pins.
splice_on_link: insert_patch_id missing, wrong exec pins, or reconnect fails after break.
Any op failure: transaction cancelled; status patch_errors, errors[], error_codes[], applied_partial always [], suggested_correct_call.
Compile errors after patch: follow with blueprint_compile for structured diagnostics.

tools.summary: Blueprint Builder sub-turn only (not default main Agent). Set one reflected property on a named Actor component on the Blueprint class default (CDO), then compile. Use for gameplay tuning that lives on component defaults (e.g. CharacterMovement.JumpZVelocity) instead of asset_apply_properties on the UBlueprint asset object. Requires blueprint_path under /Game, component name (e.g. CharacterMovement), property name (e.g. JumpZVelocity), and value (number/bool/string per property type). Does not replace blueprint_graph_patch / blueprint_graph_patch for EventGraph logic.

component.description: Component name on the class default, e.g. CharacterMovement (matches ACharacter movement component).

property.description: Property name on the component class (e.g. JumpZVelocity).

value.description: JSON value: number for floats/ints, bool, or string (enums, names, ExportText for exotic types).

tools.ue_entry_points: AActor CDO component property write, FKismetEditorUtilities::CompileBlueprint
failure_modes: Blueprint generated class is not an Actor (only Actor-based BPs have instanced component defaults on CDO).
component not found on class default, or property not editable on component instance.

tools.summary: Blueprint Builder sub-turn only (agent_surfaces: blueprint_builder). Run in-process layered layout on a Blueprint script graph (full graph or current selection). Wire knots, preserve-existing, comment synthesis, and comment reflow follow Editor Preferences → Plugins → Unreal AI Editor → Blueprint Formatting (or the Blueprint editor toolbar AI format options). Returns structured layout_* metrics. Default format_scope is full_graph.

graph_name.description: Optional; default is the first ubergraph page.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus the graph when set.

format_scope.description: full_graph: LayoutEntireGraph. selection: layout currently selected nodes in the open graph editor.

tools.summary: Blueprint Builder material_graph only: batched ops on a base Material — add_expression (class_path), delete_expression, connect (expression↔expression via pin property names), connect_material_input / disconnect_material_input (root slots e.g. BaseColor, MaterialAttributes, CustomizedUVs_0), disconnect_input, set_editor_position, set_constant3vector. Optional recompile:true runs UMaterialEditingLibrary::RecompileMaterial after ops.

ops.description: Ordered patch operations; see tool summary for op kinds.

recompile.description: When true, recompile the material after all ops (same as material_graph_compile).

failure_modes: connect: wrong to_input / from_output pin names.

tools.summary: Blueprint Builder material_graph: recompile a base Material after graph edits (UMaterialEditingLibrary::RecompileMaterial). Returns compile_error_or_pending from IsCompilingOrHadCompileError for the current RHI shader platform.
failure_modes: Shader compile errors: inspect Material Editor and adjust graph.
